name: 3D Vision · Decomposition Variant
full_intro_template: P1 Problem setup: In <3D task>, we target <deployment setting> where <hard condition> frequently appears and directly affects <downstream objective>.
P2 Why now: Recent progress in <related direction> improves <capability>, but practical systems still fail under <specific regime>.
P3 Formal gap: Existing <baseline family> assumes <assumption>, which breaks when <violation signal> occurs.
P4 Failure evidence: Prior pipelines show accumulated error from <stage-A> to <stage-B>, causing degradation on <metric>.
P5 Core idea: We decompose the full problem into <stage-1> and <stage-2> with explicit interface variable <interface token>.
P6 Mechanism detail: Stage-1 estimates <structure variable>; stage-2 refines <residual variable> with <consistency prior>.
P7 Theoretical/empirical hook: We expect monotonic improvement on <metric1> and bounded cost on <latency/memory/token>.
P8 Quantitative promise: Under matched budgets, we report <mean ± std> and absolute gain <delta%> against <strong baseline>.
P9 Boundary and failure: We include failure cases under <extreme sparsity/topology shift/motion blur> and explain failure causes.
P10 Contributions: (i) decomposition principle, (ii) implementable mechanism, (iii) diagnostics with ablation and error attribution.
abstract_template: A1: State 3D problem and deployment constraint.
A2: Identify one precise bottleneck and why prior work fails.
A3: Introduce decomposition and key mechanism.
A4: Report matched-budget improvement and one limitation boundary.
method_template: M1 Problem formulation: Define inputs, outputs, and symbols for <scene/task variable>.
M2 Pipeline overview: Describe stage-1, stage-2, and data flow between modules.
M3 Stage-1 objective: Optimize <objective-1> with constraint <constraint-1>.
M4 Stage-2 objective: Refine <objective-2> using residual signal from stage-1.
M5 Interface contract: Specify what must be passed from stage-1 to stage-2 and why.
M6 Complexity: Report training/inference overhead in <ms>, <GB>, and <FLOPs/token>.
M7 stability mechanism: Explain how <module> handles <noise/occlusion/domain shift>.
M8 Implementation notes: List key hyperparameters, seeds, and reproducibility settings.
experiments_template: E1 Datasets/protocol: Define benchmark splits, leakage checks, and matched-budget setup.
E2 Main table: Compare with strongest baselines on <metric1>, <metric2>, and <cost metric>.
E3 Statistical reliability: Report <mean ± std>, confidence interval, and significance test.
E4 Ablation-1: Remove <module-1> and quantify delta on hard subsets.
E5 Ablation-2: Remove <module-2> and analyze error propagation from stage-1 to stage-2.
E6 Efficiency: Report latency, memory, and compute trade-offs at multiple scales.
E7 Failure analysis: Provide taxonomy and representative counter-examples with explanations.
E8 Generalization: Test domain shift / out-of-distribution settings and discuss limits.
falsifiable_claims: If <core module> is removed, performance on <hard subset> should decrease by >= <delta>.
Under matched training/inference budget, <our method> should outperform <strong baseline> on <metric>.
delta_vs_strongest_baseline: Compared with <strong baseline>, we add <mechanism> and obtain <delta> under matched budget and protocol.
limitations_or_failure_cases: We explicitly analyze failure cases under <scenario> and report why the method is not dominant there.
contribution_schema: Contribution: <type> | Evidence: <verifiable artifact> | Risk/Boundary: <where it may fail>

operational_definition.B: resource or reliability metric reported jointly

paragraph_cards.template: P1 Context: <3D task> is critical for <application>, but real scenes contain <occlusion / pose variation / sparse views>.
paragraph_cards.evidence_hook: Link this paragraph to a concrete artifact: Table/Figure/Theorem/Ablation.

anti_pattern_examples.bad: Our method is reliable.

statistical_reliability_requirements: Report mean±std over >=3 random seeds when stochastic training is used.
State whether significance testing was performed and which test was used.
cost_disclosure_requirements: Report at least one cost metric from: latency, GPU memory, FLOPs.
Compare cost under matched hardware/batch settings.

reorder_profiles.description: context -> gap -> idea -> evidence -> limitation -> contributions
context -> evidence -> gap -> idea -> limitation -> contributions
context -> protocol -> gap -> idea -> evidence -> contributions

conclusion_template: C1 We summarize how decomposition from <stage-1> to <stage-2> resolves the identified gap.
C2 We highlight quantitative gains under matched settings and clarify cost boundaries.
C3 We state concrete limitations and next steps for broader deployment.
